name: General Tso's Chicken #5
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
2 Inch thick pieces fresh ginger, peeled
1/2 tbsp Chili paste (more, if you like hot food)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the chicken with the egg, oil, and cornstarch.
2. In a blender or food processor, or by hand, combine the ingredients for Sauce 1. In a small bowl, combine the ingredients for Sauce 2.
3. Place the measured ingredients beside the wok in the order in which they are to be placed in the wok.
4. Warm 2 serving dishes, one lined with paper towel, in a 250°F oven.
5. Fill the wok to a depth of 1 1/2 to 2 inches with vegetable oil. Heat the oil to high (400°F), or until a day-old cube of bread browns in just under 1 minute.
6. Heat another 4 minutes to stabilize the temperature.
7. Put the chicken pieces into the oil one at a time until all are in. Stir-fry 3 minutes.
8. Remove the pieces from the oil, draining well over the wok, and keep warm in the oven on the paper-lined dish.
9. Empty the oil from the wok. Reduce the heat to medium (350°F) and stir in Sauce 1. As soon as it bubbles, stir in the chicken.
10. Sprinkle Sauce 2 over the chicken, stir-fry 1 minute more, then turn into the serving dish.
11. Scrape the sauce over the chicken and serve at once.
